name: Implement a reentrant lock from primitives
task: Create /tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py implementing class MyRLock — a reentrant mutex. Methods: acquire() (blocking, returns when acquired) and release(). The same thread may acquire multiple times; release must be called the same number of times before another thread can acquire. A different thread calling acquire() must block until the owning thread has fully released. Build it yourself from only threading.Lock and threading.Condition together with thread-identity tracking (threading.get_ident) and a recursion counter — you may NOT use the standard library's ready-made reentrant lock.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, threading, time, tokenize, io, re
src = open('/tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py').read()
def code_only(s):
    out = []
    try:
        for tok in tokenize.generate_tokens(io.StringIO(s).readline):
            out.append(' ' if tok.type in (tokenize.COMMENT, tokenize.STRING) else tok.string)
    except Exception:
        return s
    return ' '.join(out)
c = code_only(src)
assert not re.search(r'\bRLock\s*\(', c), 'must not construct a reentrant lock from the standard library'
assert not re.search(r'(?m)^\s*from\s+threading\s+import\b[^\n]*\bRLock\b', src), 'must not import the ready-made reentrant lock'
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_rlock/rlock.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
lock = mod.MyRLock(); lock.acquire(); lock.acquire(); lock.acquire()
state = {'b': None}
t = threading.Thread(target=lambda: (lock.acquire(), state.__setitem__('b', time.monotonic()), lock.release()))
t.start(); time.sleep(0.3)
assert state['b'] is None, 'thread B got the lock too early'
lock.release(); time.sleep(0.1); assert state['b'] is None, 'still 2 acquires deep'
lock.release(); time.sleep(0.1); assert state['b'] is None
lock.release(); t.join(timeout=2)
assert not t.is_alive(), 'thread B never got the lock'
assert state['b'] is not None
print('OK')
